description.en: Korea Tourism Organization official portal. Festivals, attractions, itineraries. Includes 1330 tourist hotline info (20 languages, 24/7).
Korea Tourism Organization institutional site in English. Background on national tourism programs, press releases, official statistics.
KTO's destination marketing hub. Themed itineraries, regional highlights, official travel news in English.
Practical pre-trip info from KTO. Currency, weather, transport, etiquette, emergency contacts laid out for first-time visitors.
Korea National Tourism Organization headquarters site. Useful for confirming official events, partner businesses, certified tour operators.
KTO-affiliated traveller protection portal. Complaint filing, refund mediation, multilingual help for tourists scammed or overcharged.
KOFICE-run hub for K-pop, K-drama, K-content tourism. Concert calendars, filming locations, artist news for fans visiting Korea.
Long-running English magazine for travellers and expats in Korea. Practical city guides, restaurant reviews, weekend trip suggestions.
Korea-focused activity marketplace run from Seoul. Day tours, theme park combos, hanbok rentals booked in English.
Editorially curated city guide. Restaurants, bars, things-to-do — useful for trips beyond the usual tourist circuit.
Required for visa-free entry to Korea — apply at least 72 hours before flight. Most short-stay visitors need this.
Visa application, e-immigration, residence reporting. Includes 1345 immigration call center info.
Ministry of Education official portal for international students. D-2/D-4 visa flow, GKS scholarship applications, university directory.
Office of International Affairs. Useful template for D-2 student visa documents, immigration walk-through checklists referenced by many other universities.
Korea University international office. English visa, ARC, dorm guides commonly used as reference by other school officers.
Yonsei University international affairs. Step-by-step D-2 visa and Alien Registration Card guidance for exchange and degree students.
Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology. English admissions and visa support for STEM graduate applicants.
Government portal for non-professional E-9 work visa from 16 partner countries. Korean language test, employer matching, contract rules.
HRDK runs the EPS-TOPIK test and verifies foreign worker qualifications. Schedule, fees, exam-center directory in multiple languages.
Sogang University international site. Korean Language Education Center is one of the best-known D-4 visa sponsors for language study.
Find nearby emergency rooms, pharmacies, and English-capable hospitals. Includes 1339 medical hotline info.
Official medical tourism portal. Hospital directory, procedure guides, interpreter services for foreign patients.
Top-ranked Korean general hospital with a dedicated International Health Center. English-language appointments, interpretation, billing.
Major Seoul tertiary hospital with international health services. English appointment desk, second-opinion service, dedicated patient coordinator.
Public flagship hospital. International Healthcare Center handles English-speaking patients, visa-medical exams, second opinions.
Catholic Medical Center main hospital. Large foreign-patient department, interpretation, transplant and oncology specialties.
Drug-and-fee lookup database. Use to check whether a prescription is covered or to find certified medical institutions nationwide.
Specialty hospital network for orthopedics, joints, spine. Internationally accredited, English-speaking staff for joint-replacement tourism.
Public veterans medical center. Useful resource for retirees and long-stay foreign nationals seeking subsidized geriatric care.
Seoul National University Hospital Korean portal. Departments, doctor directory, appointment phone numbers when English site lacks detail.
Ministry of Foreign Affairs portal. Embassy contacts, consular notices, emergency line for foreign nationals.
Single sign-on portal for Korean government services. ARC reissue, certificate requests, civil complaints in English.
Korean Culture and Information Service official portal. English news on policy, K-culture, daily life — written for non-Koreans.
MOFA official site. Consular notices, treaty info, contact lists for Korean missions abroad.
Official MOEF English site. Tax policy, budget, foreign investment — relevant for long-term residents and business visitors.
Government petition and complaint portal. File grievances against agencies, propose policy. Foreign-language complaint channel available.
National Center for the Rights of the Child. Birth record requests, adoption file disclosure, post-adoption services for overseas adoptees.
Korean Statistical Information Service in English. Population, economy, prices — handy for research or relocation decisions.
NHIS English claims and premium page. Premium calculation rules, reimbursement steps, how to dispute incorrect medical bills.
Judicial system explained in English. Court hierarchy, case search, legal aid contacts for foreign litigants.
Korean railway booking. KTX, ITX, intercity. English toggle in top-right.
Flight schedules, terminal maps, baggage, transit, currency exchange. Includes call center 1577-2600.
Incheon Airport to Seoul Station direct train. Cheapest fast option, runs through the night.
The most accurate Korea map. English UI available. Better than Google Maps for transit in Korea.
Top-up and balance check for T-money transit cards. Subway / bus across Korea.
Second high-speed train operator from Suseo Station. Books faster routes to Busan, Gwangju, with English booking interface.
Nationwide express bus ticketing. Cheaper than KTX for cities without rail, English booking and seat map.
Intercity bus booking platform covering many secondary terminals not on KOBUS. Useful when destination is a smaller town.
Busan subway operator. Route map, fares, lost-and-found, accessibility info in English for Korea's second-largest city.
Daegu Metropolitan Transit Corporation. Subway lines 1-3 schedules, fares, transfer info in English.
Korea's dominant taxi-hailing app. Service overview in English, app download links. Required reading before relying on street taxis.
Real-time expressway and highway congestion, road closures, weather impact. Helpful before long road trips or rentals.
Seoul's second airport. Most domestic flights plus shuttle routes to Tokyo, Osaka, Shanghai. Terminal maps and transit info.
Seoul Metropolitan Government portal for foreign residents. Living, jobs, classes, support centers. Includes 120 Dasan Call info (5 languages).
National Health Insurance Service English guide. Mandatory enrollment for foreigners staying 6 months or longer.
Multicultural family and foreign resident portal run by the Korea Immigration Service. Counselling, life classes, multilingual helpline directory.
Official body running the Seoul Central Mosque. Prayer times, mosque locations across Korea, halal certification lookup.
Non-profit supporting Korean overseas adoptees visiting or returning to Korea. F-4 visa help, birth-family search, peer community.
Most-used Korean real estate listings. Apartments (jeonse, wolse), one-rooms, officetels with English assistance via translator browser plug-ins.
NGO supporting foreigners in Gwangju. Hotline, Korean classes, legal counselling, monthly multilingual newsletter.
Expat community resource covering school enrollment, taxes, housing, healthcare. Editorially curated for English speakers in Korea.
Korea Institute for Curriculum and Evaluation. Important for parents enrolling children in Korean K-12 — testing standards, school year info.
Example of foreigner-run business consultancy. Useful template for company setup, work visa sponsorship, accounting basics in Korea.
Naver translation. Strong for Korean-English, Korean-Japanese, Korean-Chinese. Includes camera and conversation modes.
Often more natural than Papago for long sentences and English ↔ European languages. Pair with Papago for tricky translations.
Official Korea Meteorological Administration. Typhoon, dust, heat alerts often delivered in Korean only on phones.
Korea's other major map app. Sometimes shows better business hours and reviews than Naver Map. Useful as a second opinion.
Most comprehensive Korean-English dictionary online. Phrases, hanja, example sentences. Often clearer than generic translators for nuance.
Web-based Hangul keyboard with visible key map. Type Korean without installing an IME — convenient for searching on shared devices.
Convert Korean Hangul to Romanization (Revised Romanization or McCune-Reischauer) for addresses, names, and pronunciation help.
Government road-name address lookup. Convert old Jibun addresses to new road-name format, find postal codes.
Crowdsourced free Wi-Fi locations. Useful in Korea's transit stations and cafes when your eSIM data is low or absent.
MOLIT smart-city portal. Public Wi-Fi rollout maps, IoT services, urban transit dashboards relevant to long-term residents.
Cheap international transfers. Holds KRW balance.
Cheap international remittance from Korea — popular with long-term foreign residents. Often beats banks on fees and rate.
Real-time exchange rates with KRW conversions. Handy before exchanging cash at airport or bank.
Foreigner-focused bank in Korea. English account opening, ARC required, remittance and currency exchange services.
Major Korean retail bank with foreign-employee desks at HQ and major branches. Internet banking and forex services.
One of Korea's largest banks. Shinhan SOL Global app supports English, foreign-resident accounts, salary transfer for E-class visa holders.
Top Korean fintech app. P2P transfers, bill split, simplified bank account opening once you hold an ARC.
Central bank official site. Daily exchange rates, monetary policy releases — useful before large currency conversions.
FSS English portal. File complaints against banks, brokerages, insurance providers operating in Korea. Foreigner mediation desk.
Official Korean stock exchange. Listed company info, market hours — relevant for long-stay residents managing KRW investments.
Buy a Korea data eSIM before arrival. Search 'Korea' on the home page. Works on iPhone XS+ and recent Android.
Korea-specific eSIM reseller. Multi-day data plans, instant QR delivery by email. English checkout.
Korean prepaid SIM and eSIM provider for foreigners. Airport pickup, English support, voice calls included on some plans.
SK Telecom official inbound traveller eSIM / prepaid SIM. Direct from Korea's largest carrier, English plan picker.
One of Korea's three telecom carriers. Mobile plan info and prepaid SIM lookup — pair with a Korean translator browser.
SK Telecom corporate site. Coverage info, store locator. Useful before signing a long-term postpaid contract once on ARC.
SK subsidiary running 7mobile foreigner-friendly prepaid plans. ARC-only, longer-term residents.
Aggregator selling Korean prepaid SIM and eSIM bundles for tourists. International shipping or in-airport pickup options.
Tourist eSIM with 1-30 day Korea data bundles. Instant QR delivery. English checkout, supports Apple Pay and major cards.
Reseller covering multiple Korean carriers. Pre-trip purchase with delivery to home address or Incheon pickup counter.
Korea's largest hotel booking platform with English UI. Hotels, pensions, hanok stays, motels — local inventory beyond what global sites carry.
Major Korean hotel and motel booking platform. English UI, last-minute deals, motels priced cheaper than chain hotels.
Curated Korean boutique stays — hanok, design hotels, countryside retreats. English-friendly host info.
Korea's biggest five-star chain. Seoul, Busan, Jeju, Sokcho, Pyeongchang. English booking, Signiel ultra-luxury sub-brand.
Samsung-affiliated luxury chain. Seoul, Jeju, Busan. English reservation desk, traditional Korean hospitality benchmark.
Shilla's mid-tier business-hotel brand. 20+ locations nationwide with English booking, more affordable than the flagship Shilla.
Korea Tourism Organization-certified mid-range hotel chain. Quality-vetted small hotels across the country, English booking.
Daelim mid-luxury hotel brand in Seoul (Yeouido, Gangnam, Mapo). English booking, modern business-traveller positioning.
Cultural Corps of Korean Buddhism official portal. Book a stay at 130+ certified temples — meditation, monastic meals, English programs.
Seoul Tourism Organization curated stay list. Government-vetted hotels, guesthouses, hanok stays with English contact info.
National disaster and safety portal. Earthquake, typhoon, fine dust, fire alerts. Foreign-language emergency text guidance and the 119 fire/rescue line.
Korean National Police lost-and-found database. Report or search for items lost on transit, in taxis, public spaces.
Korea Meteorological Administration English landing. Storm tracks, heat alerts, fine dust advisories. Set as your travel-day morning bookmark.
Official KMA earthquake log. Magnitude, location, time. Reference after feeling a tremor — Korea's main historical activity is on the east coast.
Korea Disease Control and Prevention Agency portal. Outbreak alerts (flu, RSV, mpox), travel-medicine guidance, vaccination schedules.
Personal Information Protection Commission. Report a leaked passport or ID, file complaints if a Korean service mishandles your data.
Korea Institute of Geoscience and Mineral Resources. Earthquake research data, fault maps, science behind tremor reports.
Public-private body promoting fire safety. Building-code basics for foreigners renting older apartments, fire-extinguisher purchase guidance.
Korean security news outlet. Track scam alerts, phishing campaigns targeting Koreans and foreigners — early warning for new fraud patterns.
Korea Occupational Safety and Health Agency. Workplace safety standards, accident reporting — critical for foreign workers on construction or factory contracts.
Seoul Metropolitan Government tourism portal. Itineraries, neighbourhoods, events, official Seoul Tourist Information Centers map.
Busan Metropolitan City tourism portal. Beaches, film, harbour itineraries, multilingual support.
Jeju Island official tourism site. Nature trails, food, no-visa entry rules for many nationalities.
Seoul city government English portal. Policy, transit, foreigner help-desk directory. Broader than Visit Seoul for residents.
Province surrounding Seoul. Suwon, Gwangmyeong, Paju — English info on attractions, business support, foreigner help centers.
Jeonju city official tourism site. Hanok Village, Korean food origins, bibimbap heritage. English itineraries and bus directions.
Major university in Daegu with strong international office. Useful regional anchor for foreigners visiting or moving to Korea's southeast.
Independent regional travel blog with vetted English itineraries by city — Andong, Sokcho, Gangneung, Tongyeong, Gyeongju.
Foreigner-run Korea travel platform. Day-trip bookings to outer Seoul (Suwon, Yongin, Pocheon) and small group experiences in English.
Anchor university in Gongju, Chungnam Province. Useful regional reference for foreigners visiting Korea's central Baekje heritage cities.
Joseon-dynasty main palace in central Seoul. English site with opening hours, ticket info, changing-of-the-guard ceremony schedule.
Indoor + outdoor theme park in Jamsil. Year-round operation. English booking, day-pass options, queue-skip pass.
Korea's largest theme park, in Yongin. Seasonal festivals, zoo safari, Caribbean Bay water park, English ticketing.
Iconic city-view tower on Namsan. Observation deck, restaurants, cable car. Best at sunset. English booking.
Official directory of palaces, UNESCO sites, traditional villages. English maps, opening hours, free-entry days.
Free flagship museum in Yongsan. English exhibits cover Korean history from prehistoric era through modern. Audio guide available.
Large indoor aquarium under COEX mall in Gangnam. Tropical, polar, river exhibits. English ticket booking, family-friendly.
Major aquarium on Haeundae Beach. Shark tunnel, penguin habitat, English ticketing — anchor activity for a Busan day trip.
Half-day trip from Seoul. Famous filming location, autumn foliage, English ferry and ticket booking — accessible by ITX-Chuncheon train.
Theme park inside Seoul Grand Park, Gwacheon. Family-friendly with Seoul Zoo and contemporary art museum within walking distance.
Yeouido's golden tower. Observation deck and art gallery on top floor, Han River views. English ticket info, less crowded than N Seoul Tower.
Samsung-founded contemporary and traditional art museum in Itaewon. Free admission to permanent collection, English signage throughout.
Concerts, K-pop, musicals, festivals. Largest Korean ticket platform with English booking.
Korean concert / musical ticketing alongside Interpark. K-pop fan-meeting and sports passes often appear here first.
Asia's largest film festival each October. Official schedule, screening tickets, accreditation info for press and industry.
Curated event calendar for Seoul. Lantern festivals, K-pop concerts, art weeks. English filters for date and category.
Books, music, and ticketing site. Niche K-pop fan events and indie concerts sometimes appear here first. Korean-only — translator browser useful.
Yes24 international storefront. K-pop CD pre-orders, fan club merchandise, English checkout shipping worldwide and to Korean hotels.
Index of certified Korean festivals nationwide. Filter by region and month — Boryeong Mud, Andong Mask, Jinju Lantern and more.
Annual Korea-wide foreigner shopping festival (Jan-Feb). Hotel discount codes, tax-refund tutorials, transit promotions.
Public broadcaster KBS World event listings. K-pop concerts, music bank tapings, fan-meet schedules announced in English.
Nami Island event calendar. Children's book festival, illustration biennale, light festivals — English schedule and ferry timing.
Long-running English-language Korean newspaper. Politics, business, K-culture. Free RSS feeds.
Korea's national news agency in English. Fastest breaking-news source for foreigners — disasters, policy changes, sports.
One of Korea's oldest English daily newspapers. Broad coverage with strong opinion section and expat community columns.
JoongAng Group English daily, partnered with NYT. Business-leaning coverage, in-depth culture and tech reporting.
Progressive English edition. Better foil to mainstream Korean media — labor, environment, North Korea perspectives.
Conservative-leaning English daily edition. Strong politics and security coverage.
Centrist Korean daily in English. Politics, economy, K-culture, plus extensive sports coverage including KBO baseball.
Public broadcaster's English service. TV news plus podcasts, K-drama recaps, language-learning programs.
Public English-language broadcaster. Live news stream, daily K-culture programming, and a strong YouTube presence.
Niche analysis service covering South Korean politics, economy, security. Heavier subscription model but free overview articles.
Maeil Business Newspaper's English service. Strong on financial markets, Korean conglomerates, semiconductor and EV industries.
